title: Luxury Ad Shot Generator
summary: Premium campaign-style image prompt for launches and ads.
use_when: Use when you need one polished hero image with commercial clarity and material realism.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when you want messy collage energy or broad ideation.
model_fit_note: Gemini performs best here because richer spatial and sensory phrasing improves image ideation quality.
proof_note: The shot gets stronger when the prompt controls hierarchy, light, texture, and exclusions.
failure_why: The prompt names the object but never controls the shot.
quick_patch: Add one contrast pair, one lens, one texture emphasis, and one exclusion list.
rare_insight: Luxury-looking output usually comes from subtraction.

compare.weak: Make a cool luxury ad for a perfume bottle.
compare.improved: Create one premium ad shot for a matte black perfume bottle on wet basalt, minimal luxury fused with raw cinematic imperfection, hard flash plus amber rim light, macro lens, controlled reflections, dense condensation, charcoal / silver / amber palette, clean silhouette, negative space for copy, avoid clutter and stock-photo feel.
why_better: Defines the shot, not just the object

example.input: Product: Nocturne fragrance bottle, matte black glass, mysterious + elite mood.
example.output: Macro ad image of a matte black fragrance bottle on wet basalt with amber rim glow and clean negative space.

default_prompt: Build one premium campaign-grade image for [SUBJECT] with one dominant focal point, scene, contrast pair, controlled lighting, camera choice, texture emphasis, palette, and exclusions. Return final image prompt + negative prompt + caption.
power_prompt: Act as a commercial creative director and campaign art supervisor. Produce one hero image concept for [SUBJECT] with one hero subject, one composition hierarchy, one emotional reaction, defined scene, light behaviour, material realism, palette, and exclusions. Return final prompt, negative prompt, and three style shifts.
minimal_prompt: Create one premium ad-style image for [SUBJECT] with a clear focal point, disciplined palette, strong lighting, tactile material realism, and no clutter. Return final prompt + negative prompt + caption.

variants.Universal: Create one premium campaign image for [SUBJECT] with clear focal hierarchy, strong light behaviour, material realism, a disciplined palette, and strict exclusions.
variants.Claude: Prioritise coherence, restraint, and hierarchy over style overload. Define scene, composition, light, lens, material realism, palette, and exclusions.
variants.Copilot: Generate a production-ready image prompt for [SUBJECT] in a premium ad style with exact subject, scene, composition, lighting, textures, palette, and exclusions.
variants.Gemini: Create a visually cinematic image prompt for [SUBJECT] with strong spatial and sensory detail, scene layout, focal hierarchy, camera perspective, light behaviour, textures, and palette.

key_improvements: Labels tell the user what to use first.
Rare insight explains why the prompt works.

title: Thumbnail That Demands a Click
summary: High-CTR thumbnail concept prompt for YouTube, reels, and social covers.
use_when: Use when one-second readability matters more than visual nuance.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when the goal is subtle branding.
model_fit_note: Copilot is strongest here because the task is packaging-heavy and benefits from explicit deliverables.
proof_note: High CTR visuals win through clarity and reaction.
failure_why: Too many visual ideas compete at once.
quick_patch: Reduce to one subject, one emotion, one visual question.
rare_insight: Bad thumbnails fail because the eye has to decode instead of react.

compare.weak: Make a viral YouTube thumbnail about AI.
compare.improved: Create a high-CTR thumbnail for a video about AI replacing jobs: one shocked face lit by screen glow, one red warning graph reflection in glasses, dark blurred office behind, strong contrast, left-side negative space for title, visual story readable in 1 second.

example.input: Topic: AI replaced my workflow in 7 days.
example.output: One shocked face, red graph reflection in glasses, dark office behind, strong contrast, clear title space.

default_prompt: Create one thumbnail concept for [TOPIC] with one dominant subject, one emotional reveal, strong contrast, clear background, one-second readability, and title-safe negative space. Return image prompt + 3 overlay options + color direction.
power_prompt: Act as a high-CTR thumbnail strategist. Optimise for one-second comprehension, one focal subject, one clear emotion, and one visual question. Specify focal subject, emotion, background simplicity, title-safe negative space, contrast logic, and anti-failure rules.
minimal_prompt: Create a thumbnail prompt for [TOPIC] with one dominant subject, one emotional cue, high contrast, clean background, and title space.

variants.Universal: Create one high-CTR thumbnail concept for [TOPIC] with one dominant subject, one emotional cue, high contrast, and title space.
variants.Claude: Design a high-CTR thumbnail for [TOPIC] with one subject, one emotion, and one visual question.
variants.Copilot: Create a thumbnail prompt for [TOPIC] with one dominant subject, simple background, strong contrast, room for text, 3 overlay options, and one sentence explaining the click logic.
variants.Gemini: Generate a click-focused thumbnail concept for [TOPIC] with strong focal subject, emotional cue, spacing for text, contrast strategy, and visual tension.

key_improvements: Variants separate packaging from visual phrasing.
Rare insight raises the prompt above generic utility.

title: Film-Still Character Builder
summary: Cinematic portrait prompt that hides biography inside visual detail.
use_when: Use when a portrait feels flat and you need implied story in the frame.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when you need clean catalogue portraiture.
model_fit_note: Claude is strongest here because the prompt depends on implication and narrative coherence.
proof_note: Hidden biography and material detail usually beat genre buzzwords.
failure_why: It relies on surface descriptors instead of history and atmosphere.
quick_patch: Add one hidden history detail, one light source, one lens, and one texture note.
rare_insight: Portrait prompts get expensive-looking when every visible choice implies history.

compare.weak: Create a cool cyberpunk woman portrait.
compare.improved: Create a prestige-film portrait of a late-30s courier in a rain-soaked retro-futurist city, stoic expression, worn leather collar, faint scar under left eye, projector-blue spill on cheekbone, 85mm close-up, visible skin texture, wet fabric fibres, restrained neon, hidden history implied by patched insignia.

example.input: Character: former underground courier turned resistance leader.
example.output: Close 85mm portrait, projector-blue spill, weathered leather collar, patched insignia, rain haze.

default_prompt: Design one unforgettable character portrait for [CHARACTER IDEA] that feels like a still from an unreleased prestige film. Include signature detail, hidden history, world context, framing, light source, atmosphere, and tactile realism.
power_prompt: Act as a prestige-film concept artist and narrative image designer. Create one portrait prompt for [CHARACTER IDEA] that implies a larger world without genre clichés. Every visual element should reveal status, history, or emotional state.
minimal_prompt: Create a prestige-film character portrait for [CHARACTER IDEA] with implied backstory, strong framing, a specific light source, and one signature detail.

variants.Universal: Create one prestige-film portrait for [CHARACTER IDEA] with implied biography, strong framing, material detail, and one defining clue.
variants.Claude: Create a cinematic portrait prompt with implied biography and world logic. Focus on posture, expression, signature detail, wear, and atmosphere.
variants.Copilot: Write a detailed portrait prompt including appearance, clothing wear, props, posture, mood, world style, lens/framing, and lighting.
variants.Gemini: Generate a visually rich portrait prompt emphasizing framing, light behaviour, atmosphere, tactile realism, and emotional tone.

key_improvements: Model variants differ by narrative restraint vs production detail vs visual richness.
Rare insight explains why implication beats genre words.

title: Executive Email Rewriter
summary: Turns rough drafts into calm, action-ready professional emails.
use_when: Use when a message is too long, defensive, unclear, or emotionally noisy.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when you have not decided what you actually need from the recipient.
model_fit_note: Copilot is strongest here because the task is workplace-structured and benefits from action formatting.
proof_note: A strong rewrite preserves the ask while removing ambiguity.
common_failure: The rewrite sounds polished but the real request becomes vague.
failure_why: The prompt optimises tone but not structure or action clarity.
quick_patch: Force the structure: context → key point → action requested → close.
rare_insight: Good rewrite prompts remove emotion first, then rebuild the message around action and consequence.

compare.improved: Rewrite this draft into a calm, concise, professional email. Preserve the actual request, remove repetition and defensiveness, structure it as context -> key point -> action requested -> close if useful, and return polished + shorter versions with 3 subject lines.
why_better: Defines structure instead of style only

example.input: Draft complaining that the order list is still not updated.
example.output: Context: the order list still misses the updated quantities. Key point: final confirmation is needed to avoid delays. Action: please confirm by tomorrow 12:00.

default_prompt: Rewrite the message below into a clear, professional email that sounds calm, competent, and easy to act on. Preserve intent, remove repetition and vagueness, and make the ask obvious. Return polished version + shorter version + 3 subject lines.
power_prompt: Act as an executive communications editor. Rewrite the draft below into a calm, competent, action-ready email. Prioritise: preserve intent, make the specific ask unmistakable, remove emotional drag, improve structure, and maintain a professional but human tone.
minimal_prompt: Rewrite this draft into a calm, concise, professional email with a clear ask.

variants.Universal: Rewrite the message into a calm, competent, action-ready email. Preserve intent, clarify the ask, remove filler, and structure the result clearly.
variants.Claude: Rewrite the message into a calm, competent, action-oriented email. Preserve intent, strip out emotional drag, clarify the main ask, and tighten structure.
variants.Copilot: Rewrite this email draft into a professional, concise, easy-to-act-on message with polished, shorter, and ultra-short versions.
variants.Gemini: Transform this draft into a concise professional email with clear action, better tone, and minimal ambiguity.

key_improvements: Rare insight makes the prompt more expert.

title: Deep Research Synthesizer
summary: Decision-oriented synthesis prompt for briefings, trade-offs, and recommendations.
use_when: Use when you need a serious briefing with knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, and an explicit recommendation.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when you only want a quick shallow summary.
model_fit_note: Claude fits best because this prompt depends on nuanced distinction, uncertainty handling, and structured reasoning.
proof_note: Research gets more trustworthy when facts, assumptions, and uncertainty are separated.
common_failure: The result becomes polished trivia instead of a decision memo.
failure_why: The prompt asks for summary but never asks for trade-offs or confidence levels.
quick_patch: Force the core question, subquestions, knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, and recommendation sections.
rare_insight: Research prompts improve when you stop optimising for completeness and start optimising for decision usefulness.

compare.improved: Build a decision-oriented research synthesis about [topic]. Define the core question, break it into 5-7 subquestions, separate high-confidence findings from contested points, map trade-offs, explain implications for [context], and end with a concise recommendation plus confidence note.

example.input: Question: Should a team standardise on one LLM or use multiple?
example.output: High confidence: models vary by strength. Trade-off: simplicity vs specialization. Recommendation: use one primary standard with defined exceptions by task.

default_prompt: Build a decision-oriented research synthesis on [TOPIC / QUESTION]. Return: core question, subquestions, knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, implications, recommendation, and confidence note.
power_prompt: Act as a lead research analyst producing a decision memo on [TOPIC / QUESTION]. Define the core question, derive subquestions, separate high-confidence findings from thin-evidence claims, identify trade-offs, explain implications, and end with recommendation plus confidence note and one thing that could change the conclusion.
minimal_prompt: Build a decision-oriented research synthesis on [TOPIC]. Return core question, knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, recommendation, and confidence note.

variants.Universal: Build a decision-oriented research synthesis on [TOPIC]. Separate facts, assumptions, and interpretation.
variants.Claude: Create a decision memo on [TOPIC / QUESTION]. Define the core question and subquestions, then separate high-confidence findings, thin-evidence claims, trade-offs, and implications.
variants.Copilot: Write a structured research synthesis about [TOPIC] with sections for core question, known facts, uncertainties, trade-offs, implications, recommendation, and confidence note.
variants.Gemini: Generate a decision-oriented research brief on [TOPIC] with clear sections for core question, knowns, unknowns, trade-offs, implications, and final recommendation.

key_improvements: Model variants differ more clearly by reasoning style.
Choice labels make the fastest valid option obvious.

title: Workflow Optimizer
summary: Operational prompt for diagnosing broken processes and designing lower-friction systems.
use_when: Use for recurring issues like unclear ownership, checklist failures, handover problems, and rework.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when the process is too vague to describe or when the problem is interpersonal rather than structural.
model_fit_note: Copilot is best for this because the task depends on ranked outputs, template fields, and practical future-state deliverables.
proof_note: Strong ops prompts rank problems by impact instead of giving generic improvement ideas.
common_failure: The model returns vague advice like communicate better.
failure_why: The prompt does not define the current-state process or output structure.
quick_patch: Describe the current workflow, demand ranked issues, and force a future-state sequence plus template fields.
rare_insight: Process prompts get sharper when you force them to distinguish structural failure from interpersonal friction.

compare.weak: How can I improve this process?
compare.improved: Analyse this workflow as an operations strategist. Identify bottlenecks, ambiguity, delays, duplicated effort, and preventable errors. Rank the top five issues by impact, propose a cleaner future-state process, separate quick wins from structural changes, and include ownership, timing, checklist fields, and implementation risks.

example.input: Current process: multiple people update an order list by email; quantities are missed; final confirmation arrives too late; no owner for changes.
example.output: Issue 1: no single owner for final quantity validation. Future state: one shared tracker, one change owner, cutoff time, standardized template.

default_prompt: Analyse this workflow and improve it. Identify friction points, bottlenecks, ambiguities, delays, duplicate effort, and preventable errors. Separate quick wins from structural changes and suggest a cleaner future-state workflow with ownership, timing, communication, checklist, and tracking fields.
power_prompt: Act as an operations strategist and process designer. Analyse the workflow below and turn it into a more reliable system. Expose hidden points of failure, rank problems by impact, separate immediate fixes from structural redesign, define ownership and timing clearly, and reduce preventable rework.
minimal_prompt: Analyse this workflow, rank the main problems by impact, propose a cleaner future-state process, and include checklist fields plus implementation risks.

variants.Universal: Analyse this workflow, rank the main issues by impact, redesign the future-state process, separate quick wins from structural changes, and include checklist fields and implementation risks.
variants.Claude: Review this workflow as an operations analyst. Diagnose current-state friction, rank the top issues by impact, and propose a cleaner future state.
variants.Copilot: Analyse this workflow and improve it. Find bottlenecks, delays, duplicate effort, missing ownership, preventable errors, and ambiguous handoffs. Return diagnosis, ranked issues, improved process, quick wins, checklist fields, and risks.
variants.Gemini: Create a future-state workflow for the process below. Identify failure points, rank issues by impact, improve the sequence, define ownership/timing/tracking, and suggest quick wins with risk controls.

key_improvements: Model variants separate operational reasoning styles more clearly.
Rare insight pushes the prompt beyond generic utility.
Tier labels tell the user the fastest best choice.
